text.title: MLP Planning Examples
text.summary: Worked examples of MVP-to-MLP upgrade paths with layer-by-layer improvements and measured outcomes.

example.note: MVP: create, edit, delete notes, basic formatting — functional but bland. NPS 30, Day-30 retention weak.

MLP additions by layer — Reliable: auto-save with visual confirmation. Usable: keyboard shortcuts, quick capture. Delight: smooth animations, satisfying micro-sounds, Zen mode for focused writing, clever empty states.
Result: retention increased 40%, NPS rose from 30 to 55.
MVP: create invoices, send to clients — functional but clunky.

MLP additions by layer — Reliable: payment confirmation notifications. Usable: templates from past invoices, client auto-fill. Delight: celebration on payment received, "You've earned $X this month" dashboard.
Result: users create 2x more invoices, NPS 60.

antipattern.description: Adding more features instead of polishing existing ones when retention is low.
antipattern.reason: More features increase scope without addressing the quality gap. Users who left due to rough UX won't return for more features with the same rough UX.

antipattern.description: Skipping MVP validation and going straight to MLP polish.
antipattern.reason: Polish on an unvalidated core value is expensive waste. Validate the job-to-be-done first, then polish the delivery.

antipattern.description: Surface-level polish (color changes, fonts) without addressing interaction quality.
antipattern.reason: Cosmetic polish without fixing interaction friction does not move retention or NPS.

antipattern.description: Generic delight not tailored to the specific audience.
antipattern.reason: Confetti on task completion delights consumer app users; it annoys enterprise finance users in the same moment. Delight must match emotional context.

antipattern.description: Adding Layer 4 delight without completing Layers 1–3.
antipattern.reason: Delight on top of unreliable or unusable flows increases frustration. Users notice the inconsistency more than the charm.
